1.Title: Response Time Spikes With Load
1.Insight: For the endpoint Homepage, response time experienced **significant spikes** when [Requests Per Second](#ClientMetrics) approached **5.0 RPS**, pointing to capacity or scaling limitations on the server.
1.Details: [Requests Per Second](#ClientMetrics) grew from 1.0 RPS at 8:51 PM UTC to a peak of **5.0 RPS at 8:53 PM UTC**. [Response Time](#ClientMetrics) fell initially to the stable range at 8:51 PM UTC–8:52 PM UTC, then soared to **3350ms at 8:52 PM UTC** and remained elevated (2310ms–2840ms) till 8:53 PM UTC. [Virtual users](#ClientMetrics) stayed constant at 10 until a drop at 8:53 PM UTC.
1.Recommendation: Consider reviewing server scalability under higher request rates. Profiling backend performance may help uncover bottlenecks in application processing or dependencies.

2.Title: Sharp Response Time Drop at Start
2.Insight: A rapid decrease in response time for [ALT-Sampler-50e95979-2244-4d11-9c38-1c03a4ccf9f3](#ResourceLink) was observed as [Requests Per Second](#ClientMetrics) increased, suggesting efficient handling of initial traffic surges.
2.Details: [Response Time](#ClientMetrics) fell dramatically from 4160ms at 8:51 PM UTC to **2300ms at 8:51 PM UTC**. [Requests Per Second](#ClientMetrics) correspondingly increased from 1.0 RPS at 8:51 PM UTC to **4.0 RPS at 8:51 PM UTC**. [Virtual users](#ClientMetrics) remained steady at 10.
2.Recommendation: Maintain server optimization for initial load burst, but monitor for degradation under sustained higher RPS to ensure consistent performance.

3.Title: Load Consistency With Late Response Time Fluctuation
3.Insight: While [Virtual users](#ClientMetrics) remained unchanged, [Requests Per Second](#ClientMetrics) and [Response Time](#ClientMetrics) for [ALT-Sampler-50e95979-2244-4d11-9c38-1c03a4ccf9f3](#ResourceLink) fluctuated, indicating inconsistent server response possibly due to backend variability or resource contention.
3.Details: [Virtual users](#ClientMetrics) held at 10 from 8:51 PM UTC to 8:53 PM UTC. [Requests Per Second](#ClientMetrics) oscillated between 3.9 RPS and 4.9 RPS, peaking at **5.0 RPS at 8:53 PM UTC** before dropping to 2.8 RPS at 8:53 PM UTC. [Response Time](#ClientMetrics) fluctuated sharply between 2310ms and 2700ms after 8:52 PM UTC.
3.Recommendation: Investigate backend service consistency and resource allocation, focusing on times of fluctuating response to optimize performance during steady-state load.

4.Summary: Load test (2 min, 10 VUs) completed with performance degradation. Response times peaked and fluctuated up to 3,350ms, with throughput varying, suggesting slow responses likely due to backend constraints.

1.Title: Response Time Fluctuates With RPS
1.Insight: For Homepage, response time exhibited cyclic peaks when requests per second increased, indicating possible backend or network throttling. Both metrics fluctuated while virtual users remained steady, suggesting the server wasn't keeping up with incoming traffic.
1.Details: [Response time](#ClientMetrics) decreased from **4160ms at 8:51 PM UTC** to 2300ms at 8:51 PM UTC, then rose to **3350ms at 8:52 PM UTC** before dropping to 2420ms at 8:52 PM UTC. [Requests per second](#ClientMetrics) rose from 1.0 RPS at 8:51 PM UTC to a peak of **5.0 RPS at 8:51 PM UTC** and 8:53 PM UTC, with dips coinciding with response time peaks. [Virtual users](#ClientMetrics) were stable at 10 until dropping to 8 at 8:53 PM UTC, showing server-side response time was the limiting factor.
1.Recommendation: Consider reviewing server resource allocation and application concurrency handling. If possible, enable server-side metrics to diagnose root causes such as CPU, memory, or database bottlenecks during load peaks.

2.Title: Request Throughput Drops at End
2.Insight: A notable decrease in request throughput for [ALT-Sampler-05bff399-db67-47a4-b930-dc370c8bbacc](#ResourceLink) occurred at the end of the test, while virtual users dropped only slightly, suggesting a bottleneck or throttling event.
2.Details: [Requests per second](#ClientMetrics) dropped sharply from **5.0 RPS at 8:53 PM UTC** to 2.8 RPS at 8:53 PM UTC. [Virtual users](#ClientMetrics) decreased from 10 to 8 only at 8:53 PM UTC. [Response time](#ClientMetrics) increased to 2700ms at 8:53 PM UTC, indicating server or network saturation impacting throughput.
2.Recommendation: Investigate server-side logs and resource utilization around 8:53 PM UTC for possible saturation or throttling events. If this pattern repeats, consider scaling backend resources or optimizing request processing logic at peak times.

3.Summary: Load test (2 min, 10 VUs) completed with performance degradation. Response times fluctuated with frequent spikes and throughput dips, suggesting backend latency under load; root cause unclear due to missing server metrics.
